unit.subject: English
unit.unitOverview: In this unit, your child learns the words for shapes, colours, and clothes, and how to make things with paper shapes — like a paper quilt, a picture, or a shape animal. They talk about what people are wearing and dress up in pretend party costumes. They meet the short ‘e’ sound (hen, pen, tent) and enjoy the old story of the elves and the shoemaker. The gentle idea running through it all is that making things takes creativity and patience — and it is fun to try, try again.
unit.learningPath:
Begin with the guided teacher launch and learn a few words through pictures and play.
Listen to the Unit story and join in with repeated language.
Complete six language, speaking, early-writing and practical activities.
Meet your teacher three times each week for two weeks.
Finish the ten-question checkpoint and tell an adult what you can do.

readings.title: Amal Makes a Mat
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
It was a warm afternoon in the village. Amal sat with her grandmother, Ayeeyo, under the big tree.

Ayeeyo was making a mat from soft grass. Her hands moved fast. Over, under, over, under.

“I want to make a mat too!” said Amal.

“Good,” said Ayeeyo. “Here is some grass. We will make it together.”

Selma took some grass. She started to weave. Over, under, over, under.

But her mat was not neat. It was bumpy and full of holes.

“Oh no!” said Amal. “My mat is not good.”

Her big brother Ishmael walked by. He was carrying paper and crayons.

“Do not be sad,” said Ishmael. “Look, I am making a picture with shapes. I cut a red circle and a blue square.”

He showed Amal his paper. There was a green triangle and a yellow rectangle too.

“It is beautiful,” said Amal. “But my first picture was not so good either!” said Ishmael. He laughed. “I tried again and again.”

Selma looked at her bumpy mat. She took a deep breath.

“I will try again,” she said.

Over, under, over, under. Slow and careful this time. Amal did not rush.

Little by little, the mat grew. The holes were gone. It was flat and neat.

“Ayeeyo, look!” said Amal.

Ayeeyo smiled a big smile. “You did it, Amal! You were patient. That is how we make good things.”

Selma held up her mat. It was red and brown, like the earth.

Her little sister Samira clapped her hands. “I want to make a mat too!”

“Come,” said Amal. “I will help you. Over, under, over, under…”

And under the big tree, the whole family sat and made things together, all afternoon long.

readings.title: Talk about Making Things
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript: Look, point and talk. This is a ___. It is a ___ ___. I am cutting ___. I am making ___. I am wearing ___. I can be a ___. An adult reads each line while the learner points, repeats and acts.

readings.title: Making Things rhyme
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
The “Party Time” poem — say it together
Party time,  /  Look at me.  /  What do you see?  /  I can be a clown  /  With a funny frown.  /  Or a king  /  With a silver ring.
Act it out! Make a funny frown for the clown. Put on a pretend crown for the king. Say it three times — a little sillier each time!
The “Higgledy Piggledy” rhyme — for the short ‘e’ sound
Higgledy Piggledy,  /  my black hen.  /  She lays eggs  /  for gentlemen.  /  Sometimes nine and sometimes ten.
Listen for the ‘e’ sound in hen, eggs, gentlemen and ten. Flap your arms like a hen while you say it!

grammar.title: Name your craft materials
grammar.explanation: Pick up one thing from your craft box and name it. Hold it high as you speak.
grammar.commonMistake: Children sometimes say only the object name. Ask for the whole sentence, starting 'This is a...'.
grammar.memoryTip: Lift the object up high when you say the word 'This'.
grammar.practice: Name three craft things: This is a box. This is a straw. This is a stick.

grammar.title: Put the describing word first
grammar.explanation: Tell us more about your material. In English the describing word comes before the thing.
grammar.commonMistake: 'It is a box big' is a common slip. In English we say the describing word first: 'a big box'.
grammar.memoryTip: Describing word first, thing second: big then box, red then straw.
grammar.practice: Describe three materials: It is a big box. It is a red straw. It is a soft cloth.

grammar.title: Say what you are cutting
grammar.explanation: While you use your safe scissors, tell your teacher what you are cutting at this moment.
grammar.commonMistake: Some children say 'I cutting paper.' Keep 'am' in the middle: 'I am cutting paper.'
grammar.memoryTip: Snip two fingers like scissors as you say the word 'cutting'.
grammar.practice: Say three as you work: I am cutting paper. I am cutting card. I am cutting string.

grammar.title: Tell everyone what you are making
grammar.explanation: Show your work and say what it will be. Use this while you are still busy building it.
grammar.commonMistake: Children may say 'I making a card.' The small word 'am' belongs in every sentence like this.
grammar.memoryTip: Say it while your hands are still busy, because 'making' means not finished yet.
grammar.practice: Say three about your work: I am making a card. I am making a lantern. I am making a boat.

grammar.title: Say what you are wearing
grammar.explanation: Look down at your clothes today and say one thing you have on.
grammar.commonMistake: 'I am wearing a shoes' mixes 'a' with a plural. Say 'a shoe' for one and 'shoes' for two.
grammar.memoryTip: Touch each piece of clothing as you name it.
grammar.practice: Name three clothes you have on: I am wearing a shirt. I am wearing shoes. I am wearing a cap.

grammar.title: Say the job you dream of
grammar.explanation: Dream a little about the future. Name a job you would love to do one day.
grammar.commonMistake: Some children leave out 'a': 'I can be builder.' Job words need 'a' in front of them.
grammar.memoryTip: Act out the job while you say it: build, bake, teach.
grammar.practice: Say three jobs you would enjoy: I can be a builder. I can be a doctor. I can be a teacher.

speaking.title: Speaking 1 - Listen and point
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: This is a ___. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern This is a ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 2 - Repeat the model
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: It is a ___ ___. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern It is a ___ ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 3 - Ask and answer

speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern I am cutting ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 4 - Picture talk

speaking.title: Speaking 5 - Role-play

speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern I am wearing ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 6 - My confident turn

speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern I can be a ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

writing.title: Writing 1 - Draw and tell
writing.promptAndInstructions: Draw one idea about shapes, clothes, creativity and patience. Tell an adult what it shows.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 2 - Trace
writing.promptAndInstructions: Trace two useful words, saying each sound or word as you trace.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 3 - Match and copy
writing.promptAndInstructions: Match three words to pictures, then copy one word.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 4 - Label
writing.promptAndInstructions: Add two labels to your drawing or picture.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 5 - Complete a pattern
writing.promptAndInstructions: Copy and complete: I am wearing ___.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 6 - Create one sentence
writing.promptAndInstructions: Choose a picture and complete one short sentence using: I can be a ___.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

activities.title: Activity 1
activities.instructionsAndItems: 1.  Colour the shapes. Colour the circle red, the square blue, the triangle green, the rectangle yellow. Say each shape name!
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 2
activities.instructionsAndItems: 2.  Draw something YOU made. Then say: “I made a ______.”
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 3
activities.instructionsAndItems: 3.  Match the maker to what they make. Draw a line from each child to their shape.
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 4
activities.instructionsAndItems: 4.  Trace your name on your work. Then draw a happy face next to it!
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 5
activities.instructionsAndItems: 5.  Point and say. Point to each thing and say “I can wear a ______.”
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 6
activities.instructionsAndItems: 6.  What can you make? Draw yourself making it, then say “I can make a ______.”
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

assignments.title: My Making Things show-and-tell page
assignments.instructions: Submit one drawing or made object, two labels or traced words, one recorded spoken pattern and an adult-supported reflection.
assignments.submissionType: Drawing or object + labels + short audio/video + reflection

liveSessions.title: Meet the words
liveSessions.beforeSession: Bring crayons and one real object connected to the Unit.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min hello song; 7 min model and repeat; 8 min game or story; 7 min guided practice; 3 min celebrate and preview
liveSessions.afterSession: Show one word, action, drawing or sentence from Making Things to an adult.

liveSessions.title: Story and talk
liveSessions.beforeSession: Complete the previous short practice with an adult.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min hello song; 7 min model and repeat; 8 min game or story; 7 min guided practice; 3 min celebrate and preview

teacherNotes.note: Use 15-20 minute independent blocks inside the lesson. Read every instruction aloud, model before expecting a response, and accept pointing, acting, drawing, tracing or speaking as valid evidence.
A teacher or caregiver remains present whenever the young learner uses recording or AI tutor features. The adult controls the device and protects the child's personal information.

selfAssessment.statement: I can name and point to shapes (square, circle, triangle, rectangle).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can name and point to colours (red, blue, green, yellow, silver, gold…).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can name some clothes and costumes (hat, shirt, dress, clown, king…).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can say “I’m cutting ______.” and “I’m making a ______.”.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can say “I’m wearing ______.” to describe party clothes.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can hear and say words with the short ‘e’ sound (hen, pen, ten, wet).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself
